question_text: Which of the following describes what happens to the particles when ice melts into water?

options.label: A
options.text: The particles break apart completely and form a gas

options.label: B
options.text: The particles gain energy and move more freely, but stay bonded to nearby particles

options.label: C
options.text: The particles lose energy and move closer together

options.label: D
options.text: The number of particles in the ice decreases

explanation: During melting, particles gain thermal energy, which allows them to move more freely and overcome some of the forces holding them in fixed positions. However, they remain attracted to nearby particles, which is why the substance is still a liquid (not a gas). The total number of particles stays the same because melting is a physical change.
